title: Syzygium escritorii
intro: Syzygium escritorii, also known as the Mindanao water apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium escritorii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Syzygium escritorii is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.

The plant is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves of Syzygium escritorii are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, while the bark is used to treat diarrhea and other gastrointestinal problems.
